mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over recent months lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy is actively easing.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.50 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 20d returns more often than negative returns, or if the 20d hit rate stays below 55% across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market stress and weaker household income expectations, which should pressure equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-spike episodes are followed by positive 20d S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS together with rising PAYEMS indicates broad nominal demand and improving corporate revenue prospects, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered by simultaneous RSAFS and PAYEMS acceleration it fails to yield positive 20d returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in INDPRO signals recovering industrial output and tighter supply utilization, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60d forward returns after two-month INDPRO increases are not above baseline, or if the signal's sign flips negative in more than one rolling 24-month subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both break out after weakness, the economy is transitioning into a cyclical rebound that improves sales and housing-linked demand, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS each rise month-over-month after at least 3 months of flat or declining values
failure_condition: Reject if the combined breakout condition does not yield a higher 60d return rate than either proxy individually, or if conditional positive-return frequency stays below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation readings combined with an unchanged or rising FEDFUNDS rate imply real policy tightening, which compresses equity multiples and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds the FEDFUNDS rate
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes do not have below-baseline 20d returns, or if the signal remains negative despite policy-rate easing when inflation is elevated.

mechanism_narrative: A low but stable UNRATE during a gradual PAYEMS recovery identifies a durable reopening phase, and equities should continue higher over the next 5 trading days as recession fears fade.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-recovery state fails to produce positive 5d returns more often than 52% of the time, or if the effect disappears once PAYEMS stops rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a sharp increase in HOUST relative to the prior quarter signals stronger construction demand and a broader cyclical pickup, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 10% over 3 months after a prior trough
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound episodes do not outperform baseline 60d returns, or if GDP-confirmed recoveries do not improve the signal's hit rate above 55%.
